'''Kyle Trout''' (born 1 March 1991) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]] in the [[Betfred Championship]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr|lf}}.

He played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], playing on loan from the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|Wildcats]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], [[Batley Bulldogs]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has also played for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Trout was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He is the older brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Owen Trout]].

He played as a youth for [[Stanley Rangers]].

In February 2021 he revealed he has [[autism]] and [[panic attacks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-kyle-trout-autism-5003602 |title=Ex-Hull KR forward Kyle Trout opens up on battle with autism and panic attacks during games |publisher=Hull Live |date=15 February 2021}}</ref>

==Club career==
On 5 August 2019, he signed a contract with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for the remainder of the season. Trout then went on to sign a further one-year contract with the [[Super League]] outfit, keeping him at the club until the end of the 2020 season.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Hull KR===
Hull KR released Trout at the end of the 2020 season.

===Limoux Grizzlies===
On 7 January 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Limoux Grizzlies]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Jan 2021|access-date=15 Jan 2021|title=Trout heads to Limoux|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/french-season-resumes-this-weekend-presidents-international-plans-fages-is-top-dog/}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
On 10 June 2021, he signed for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Jun 2021|accessdate=11 Jun 2021|title=Newcastle snap up Mitch Clark and Kyle Trout|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-snap-up-mitch-clark-and-kyle-trout/}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers (Re-join)===
On 1 December 2021, he signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Dec 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Featherstone bring back former forward|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-bring-kyle-trout-2022/}}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars===
On 29 June 2022  he  signed for [[Keighley Cougars]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|accessdate=29 Jun 2022|title=Kyle Trout reeled in by League 1 promotion-chasers Keighley|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/kyle-trout-reeled-in-by-league-1-promotion-chasers-keighley/}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
===Hunslet RLFC===
On 17 July 2025 he was appointed head-coach for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Hunslet RLFC|date=17 Jul 2025|accessdate=31 Jul 2025|title=Kyle Trout appointed Head Coach|url=https://hunsletrlfc.com/team/kyle-trout-appointed-head-coach/}}</ref>
